bibliography.title: Borane-Catalyzed Enantioselective α-Alkylation of Unactivated 2-Alkylbenzoxazoles with Electron-Deficient Olefins
bibliography.venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

active_catalyst_state.paraphrase: The supporting information reports in situ CAT2 generation and a nonlinear catalyst-ee/product-ee relationship, but it does not identify a unique CAT2 aggregation or substrate-bound active state.

alternative_pathway.paraphrase: The reviewed SI record contains BF3 and B(C6F5)3 mechanistic submodels, but this smoke review did not find an exhaustive comparison of alternative CAT2 pathways.

barrier_or_energy_profile.paraphrase: Figure S4 reports 9.2 kcal mol-1 for BF3-TS1 and 16.9 kcal mol-1 for the BF3-TS2-B1/B2 C-C-forming topologies; the exact common energy-zero definition remains unresolved.

computational_protocol.paraphrase: The SI reports Gaussian 09, omega-B97XD/6-31G(d) optimization and frequency calculations with SMD, followed by omega-B97XD/def2-TZVPP single points with SMD at 298.15 K. Solvent identity, full route options, standard state, and low-frequency treatment are not resolved in the reviewed record.

coordinates.paraphrase: Cartesian coordinates are reported for BF3-TS1 and the BF3-TS2-B1/B2 models; these are achiral Lewis-acid submodels rather than CAT2 structures.

elementary_step.paraphrase: The source reports a BF3-associated proton-transfer TS from 2-ethylbenzoxazole to Barton's base and subsequent BF3-associated C-C-forming topologies with tert-butyl acrylate.

irc.paraphrase: The SI states generally that IRC calculations were used to determine connectivity of minima and transition states, but candidate-specific directions and identified endpoint structures are not reported in the reviewed record.

normal_mode.paraphrase: Figure S4 associates featured imaginary frequencies of -1455.35, -389.10, and -351.55 cm-1 with BF3-TS1, BF3-TS2-B1, and BF3-TS2-B2. This review did not inspect animated modes, so reaction-coordinate validation remains unresolved.

proposed_mechanism.paraphrase: The source reports proton transfer followed by C-C bond formation in BF3 and B(C6F5)3 computational submodels. It does not establish that this achiral sequence is the operative CAT2 active-state mechanism.

selectivity_model.paraphrase: The source reports product 4a in 86% isolated yield and 92% ee and gives a nonlinear-effect series, but it does not report a CAT2 transition-state ensemble that quantitatively models enantioselectivity.

transition_state_model.paraphrase: The SI reports BF3-TS1 and the achiral BF3-TS2-B1/B2 topology pair. These structures can inform step topology only and are not CAT2 enantioselectivity transition states.

source_locations.locator: SI S76-S77, Figure S4; coordinates S116-S121

reported_protocol.solvation: SMD; solvent identity unresolved in the reviewed method record

irc_directions_reported: general IRC connectivity statement only; directions and endpoints are not candidate-specific
reported_ts_path.model_truncations: BF3 and B(C6F5)3 achiral Lewis-acid submodels; not a CAT2 active-state or enantioselectivity ensemble
reported_ts_path.normal_mode_interpretation: Featured imaginary frequencies are source-reported, but animated normal modes were not inspected in this review.

reviewer_decision.rationale: The DOI and version-matched SI establish the exact experimental reaction and report achiral BF3/BCF elementary-step and TS topologies. Because the primary article full text was not accessible in this smoke and the computed catalyst model is not CAT2, the record is retained as source-checked background for topology only, not as a direct CAT2 mechanism or selectivity precedent.

access_notes: OpenAlex live metadata resolved DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067 and reported is_retracted=false.
ACS Figshare API resolved version-1 supporting information DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067.s001, resource DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067, file ja4c09067_si_001.pdf, and CC BY-NC 4.0 metadata.

The publisher article full text was not accessible in this smoke; article-level support is limited to the DOI record, abstract evidence preserved in forward-study.json, and the version-matched SI review preserved in repository records.
No PDF was downloaded during this smoke; the repository preserves the previously reviewed SI SHA-256 record only.

bibliography.title: Borane Exchange Enables Photocatalytic Alkylation of Pyridylic C(sp 3 )−H Bonds with Olefins

reviewer_decision.rationale: Live metadata screening identifies a photocatalytic pyridylic C(sp3)-H alkylation rather than the chiral-bisborane-catalyzed alpha-alkylation of 2-alkylbenzoxazoles. Its low lexical score and remote transformation, substrate, catalyst, and stereochemical dimensions justify exclusion without promoting any mechanism or TS claim.

access_notes: OpenAlex live metadata resolved DOI 10.1002/cjoc.70375 and reported is_retracted=false.
Full article and supporting information were not opened because the metadata-level transformation and substrate mismatch was sufficient for exclusion.
